metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove or qualify the 'Confirmed compatible with xdist' assertion in the Summary since neither `gerrit-verify.yaml` nor `test.yml` currently use xdist (`-n` flag); if xdist compatibility was genuinely verified, cite the specific test run or condition under which it was tested.
findings.finding: The plan asserts '--durations' is 'Confirmed compatible with xdist' but neither workflow file uses xdist (-n flag is absent from both pytest invocations in gerrit-verify.yaml and test.yml). The xdist compatibility claim is untestable against the actual gate configuration and may be a stale assertion from a different environment.
findings.location: Summary section: 'Confirmed compatible with `-q`, coverage, and xdist.'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'Confirmed compatible with `-q`, coverage, and xdist' to 'Confirmed compatible with `-q` and coverage (the current gate flags); xdist compatibility is not exercised by the current gate.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the `--store-durations` scheduled step is IN scope for this task or explicitly a follow-on ticket; state the default (e.g., 'Out of scope for this change — implement as a separate follow-up').
findings.finding: The OPTIONAL `--store-durations` step is listed in the 'What' section as in-scope prose but tagged 'OPTIONAL (behind this task)' — an executing agent cannot determine whether to implement it in this task or treat it as a follow-on, creating a defaultable scope gap.
findings.location: What section, second bullet: 'OPTIONAL (behind this task): add a scheduled/post-merge step...'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'OPTIONAL (behind this task)' with: 'OUT OF SCOPE — tracked as a follow-up task. If it is added later, the file must be artifact/gitignored (AC #3 applies).' This makes the executor's decision unambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC sub-step (or note in the What section) that `.test_durations` must be added to the repo-root `.gitignore` before or alongside the `--store-durations` step, since it is currently absent from `.gitignore` and its presence in the working tree would trigger the session-level repo-isolation guard in `tests/conftest.py`.
findings.finding: The plan's AC requires '.test_durations must be gitignored (or artifact-only)' and claims it 'never leaks into the checkout and never trips the repo-leak guard' — but '.test_durations' is not present in the repo's .gitignore today, and no AC step specifies adding the gitignore entry as a concrete deliverable. The conditional 'If a .test_durations store step is added' in AC item 3 means this work could be done without the gitignore entry, creating a window where the file trips the session-level isolation guard.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria item 3: 'If a `.test_durations` store step is added, the file is artifact/gitignored (never committed, never leaks into the working tree).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC item 3: 'and `.test_durations` is added to the repo-root `.gitignore` (currently absent) so the session-level isolation guard in `tests/conftest.py` does not fire.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC and 'What' section to also add `--durations=25` to the integration-tier pytest invocation in both `gerrit-verify.yaml` and `test.yml`, or explicitly justify why the integration tier is intentionally excluded.
findings.finding: The AC and 'What' section target only the 'default-suite pytest command' in each workflow file, but both `gerrit-verify.yaml` and `test.yml` contain a second, separate pytest invocation for the integration tier (lines 355-358 and 374-380 respectively). Slow integration tests will remain unobservable in CI, undercutting the stated goal that 'slow tests are invisible until someone profiles by hand' is resolved.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (AC line 1) and What section; `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` lines 355-358; `.github/workflows/test.yml` lines 374-380
findings.suggested_fix:
In both `gerrit-verify.yaml` and `test.yml`, add `--durations=25` to the integration-tier step as well:
```yaml
pytest -m integration -q -p no:cacheprovider \
  --basetemp="${{ runner.temp }}/rebar-basetemp-int" \
  --durations=25
```
Alternatively, update the AC to clarify that the integration tier is intentionally excluded and explain why.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either remove the `--store-durations` / `pytest-split` sharding prose from the plan entirely (defer to a separate ticket when CI slowness is actually measured), or demote it to a 'future work' appendix with an explicit 'not part of this deliverable' marker so implementers don't build it now.
findings.finding: The OPTIONAL `--store-durations` / artifact-upload / future pytest-split sharding step is described as a concrete deliverable ('add a scheduled/post-merge step…') but is backed by zero cited evidence of CI slowness, profiling data, or a sharding requirement — making it premature-optimization per the G-9 scale-calibration rule. `pytest-split` is not installed (absent from pyproject.toml `[dev]`), so 'Confirmed compatible with xdist' is also unverifiable in the current environment.
findings.location: Plan § What — second bullet; § Acceptance Criteria — third AC line
findings.suggested_fix: Move the second bullet of § What and the third AC line into a clearly-labeled 'Future / not-in-scope' section, and add a note: 'Revisit when CI gate P95 exceeds N minutes (measure first)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `pytest-split` (pinned version) to the `[dev]` optional-dependencies in `pyproject.toml`, or explicitly note in the plan that the OPTIONAL step requires a separate install step and update the AC to reflect the install requirement.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `--store-durations` is 'verified working on this suite,' but `pytest-split` (the package that provides this flag) is not declared in the project's `[dev]` optional-dependencies in `pyproject.toml`. A fresh CI runner installing `pip install -e '.[dev]'` will not have `pytest-split`, so the OPTIONAL scheduled step will fail with an 'unrecognized arguments' or plugin-not-found error the moment it is wired up.
findings.location: What section ('--store-durations --durations-path .test_durations … Verified: --store-durations writes a valid JSON map'); pyproject.toml [project.optional-dependencies.dev]
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"pytest-split>=0.9"` (or the exact version verified) to the `dev` extra in `pyproject.toml` alongside `pytest>=8` and `pytest-cov>=5`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `--store-durations` and `--durations-path` are the exact flag names in the installed pytest-split version by running `pytest --help | grep -E 'store-durations|durations-path'` and record the output as evidence in the plan before implementing the optional step.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `--store-durations` and `--durations-path` as valid pytest-split CLI flags ('Verified: --store-durations writes a valid JSON map') but provides no observable evidence (--help output, log snippet, or in-repo invocation) confirming these exact flag names exist in the installed version of pytest-split. The plugin is installed but the specific flag surface is unverified at flag-name granularity — a flag-name mismatch (e.g. if the flag were `--durations-output` or version-gated) would cause a silent CI failure on the optional step.
findings.location: What section — 'OPTIONAL (behind this task): add a scheduled/post-merge step that runs `pytest --store-durations --durations-path .test_durations`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single-line evidence snippet to the plan's 'What' section: run `pytest --help | grep durations` on the target runner and paste the matching lines. If the flags differ from what the plan states, update the flag names accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC #2, such as: `grep -A 5 'slowest.*durations' <ci-log-file>` or instruct reviewers to search the workflow run log for pytest's `=== slowest 25 durations ===` section.
findings.finding: AC #2 ('A gate run's log shows the 25 slowest tests') lacks a concrete proving command; there is no specified grep pattern or log anchor to confirm the durations section appears, leaving verification open to subjective log inspection.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC #2: 'Verified by searching the CI run log for the line matching `slowest 25 durations` (e.g., `grep -c "slowest 25 durations" run.log` returns 1).`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify failure behavior for the `--store-durations` artifact upload step (e.g., `continue-on-error: true` with an explicit warning log, or an alert) so upload failures are visible rather than silently dropped.
findings.finding: The optional `--store-durations` artifact upload step has no error-handling or observability specification: if the upload fails (CI storage quota, network blip, permissions), the failure mode is unspecified and not instrumented.
findings.location: What section — optional store-durations step / AC: 'uploads .test_durations as an artifact'
findings.suggested_fix: In the workflow YAML, add `continue-on-error: true` to the artifact upload step and immediately follow it with a step that checks the outcome and logs a warning (e.g., `if: steps.upload.outcome == 'failure' → echo 'WARNING: durations artifact upload failed'`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the full lifecycle for `.test_durations`: specify artifact retention duration (e.g., `retention-days: 30`), enumerate which steps/jobs consume it and how they tolerate absence or staleness, and document the cleanup/retirement policy.
findings.finding: The `.test_durations` shared-state file has no defined RETIRE/cleanup lifecycle: artifacts accumulate in CI storage indefinitely with no specified retention policy, and consumers of the file are not enumerated — only vaguely referenced as 'the team later' with pytest-split.
findings.location: What section — optional store-durations step; AC: '.test_durations must be gitignored (or artifact-only)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `retention-days: 30` (or similar) to the `actions/upload-artifact` call; add a prose note in the plan stating that the sharding consumer (pytest-split) must handle a missing file gracefully (e.g., fall back to equal splitting); and document that old artifacts are auto-expired by the retention setting.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
